_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Add OpenCode stub to worktree E2E stack

steps.description: scripts/e2e_opencode_stub.py — minimal SSE/HTTP server mimicking opencode v1.15.0: 9 HTTP endpoints + /event SSE stream; HTTP Basic auth (opencode:$OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD); CLI `serve --hostname H --port N`; deterministic synthetic event stream on prompt_async; ring buffer (deque maxlen=256) for Last-Event-ID replay. TDD-RED first via direct httpx integration tests.

Dockerfile.e2e — add a /usr/local/bin/opencode shim that execs the stub via `uv run python /app/scripts/e2e_opencode_stub.py "$@"`. Verify .opencode/config.json permission block is COPYed in. Document the stub-vs-real-binary trade-off in a comment. Keep added layer count to ≤ 1.

docker-compose.e2e.yml — add IW_CORE_OPENCODE_BIN=/usr/local/bin/opencode and IW_CORE_OPENCODE_PORT="4096" to e2e-dashboard.environment. Extend the dashboard healthcheck to gate on /api/chat/config returning 200.

tests/integration/test_e2e_opencode_stub.py — spawn the stub subprocess on a free port; verify every endpoint shape, basic-auth enforcement, SSE event sequence on prompt_async, Last-Event-ID replay, permission allow/deny follow-up events. Per tests/CLAUDE.md: no live-DB connection; assertion-strength enforced by make test-assertions.

docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md — append "E2E OpenCode stub" subsection: purpose, scope, why a stub vs the real binary, how to extend the event vocabulary for richer chat-related browser_verification needs.

Per-agent review of S01–S05. CRITICAL on: stub binding to non-loopback; basic-auth bypass; secret leakage in logs; new pyproject.toml dependencies; modifications to production orch/chat/** or dashboard/routers/chat.py; image-build network-fetch in offline conditions. HIGH on: missing ring-buffer for /event replay; HTTP shape divergence from OpencodeClient's expectations; healthcheck regression; image size grew >50 MB; assertion-weakness in tests.
Apply CRITICAL/HIGH fixes from S06. Re-run only the targeted tests for files touched. Do NOT run make test-* — those are QV gates.

Cross-agent final review. Independent reruns: targeted integration tests; visual inspection of git diff --stat against scope.allowed_paths; pre-confirm what S15 (test-integration) will run by running it once locally. Verify Dockerfile.e2e change adds no heavy install steps (build time stays under ~3 min).
Apply CRITICAL/HIGH fixes from S08. If S08 was clean, this step is a no-op — write a brief report and pass through.

QV: Browser verification — pre-flight /api/chat/config → 200 with non-empty models array; V2 Ctrl+/ toggles chat panel; V3 send a prompt and verify deterministic synthetic streaming; V4 permission.asked approval modal renders + allow/deny round-trips; V_no_regressions covers other dashboard pages + existing right-side Code Q&A regression guard.

Self-assessment of CR-00054 via iw-item-analyze. Focus: did fix-cycles burn on stub HTTP-shape mismatches vs OpencodeClient; was the /event ring-buffer Last-Event-ID path tested deeply enough; did S18 surface chat UX gaps that the deterministic stub can't simulate; cross-item pattern signals shared with F-00083's self-assess.
